name: Bluevine SMB Banking and Credit API
what_it_does: Bluevine SMB banking and business credit REST API for small and medium businesses to access business checking, lines of credit, and financial services through a digital-first platform. Enables AI agents to manage business checking account and high-yield savings for SMB financial operations automation, handle line of credit draws and repayments for working capital automation, access invoice financing and factoring for accounts receivable acceleration automation, retrieve real-time account balance and transaction monitoring for cash flow automation, manage business debit card and spend controls for expense management automation, handle ACH and wire transfers for business payment automation, access bill payment and vendor management for AP automation, retrieve business credit limit management and utilization for credit monitoring automation, manage sub-accounts for business unit financial separation automation, and integrate Bluevine with accounting software and business tools for connected SMB financial management.
use_cases: Managing business checking from SMB cash management agents
Drawing lines of credit from working capital automation agents
Accelerating receivables from invoice financing agents
Monitoring credit utilization from business credit management agents
not_for: Enterprise treasury management (Bluevine targets SMB under $10M revenue)
Commercial real estate lending (use commercial bank CRE products)
International banking with multi-currency accounts (US-focused platform)
best_when: A small or medium business wanting AI agents to automate business checking management, working capital credit draws, invoice financing, and payment operations within Bluevine's SMB banking and credit platform.
avoid_when: LINE OF CREDIT COVENANT MONITORING FOR AUTOMATED DRAWS: Bluevine lines of credit have eligibility requirements (minimum revenue, account age, payment history); automated credit draws may be declined if eligibility factors change; automated working capital draw workflows must verify credit availability and eligibility before automated draw to avoid failed draw at critical cash flow moment. INVOICE FACTORING RECOURSE vs NON-RECOURSE RISK: Bluevine invoice factoring terms may include recourse provisions for non-payment by invoice debtor; automated invoice factoring without understanding recourse terms creates financial liability if factored invoices go uncollected; automated factoring deployment requires legal review of recourse terms. HIGH-YIELD SAVINGS RATE VARIABILITY: Bluevine business savings account interest rate is variable and adjusts with Fed Funds rate; automated treasury management optimization that assumes fixed Bluevine savings rate creates yield forecast variance; implement rate monitoring for automated treasury allocation decisions.

auth.notes: Bluevine uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. REST API with JSON. Redwood City, California HQ. Founded 2013 by Eyal Lifshitz and Nir Klar. Backed by Menlo Ventures, ION Crossover Partners, Citi Ventures ($770M+ raised). Banking partner: Coastal Community Bank (FDIC member) for deposit products. Products: Business checking, high-yield savings (5%+ APY at times), line of credit ($250K max), invoice factoring. 500,000+ SMB customers. US-only operations. Competes with Mercury, Relay, and Novo for SMB neobanking; OnDeck and Kabbage for SMB credit.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free business checking with no monthly fees; line of credit has draw fee or interest
pricing.paid_tiers: Free business checking; line of credit interest (variable APR based on creditworthiness); invoice factoring fee percentage
pricing.notes: Redwood City CA. Menlo Ventures, Citi Ventures backed. Free checking. LOC interest on draws. Invoice factoring fee.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; account and credit queries throttled; webhooks for transaction, payment, and credit events

known_agent_gotchas: LINE OF CREDIT AVAILABLE BALANCE vs APPROVED LIMIT DISTINCTION: Bluevine line of credit shows both approved limit and available balance (approved minus outstanding draws); automated working capital draw logic must use available balance, not approved limit, to avoid over-draw attempt; available balance is lower than approved limit when outstanding draws exist

CREDIT UNDERWRITING PERIODIC REVIEW IMPACT ON AUTOMATED DRAWS: Bluevine periodically reviews credit eligibility and may adjust credit limit or suspend access; automated credit draw workflows must handle credit limit reduction or suspension events; automated draw scheduling without credit availability check creates failed draw at cash flow critical moment if limit was reduced since last check

INVOICE FACTORING DEBTOR CREDITWORTHINESS VERIFICATION: Bluevine invoice factoring evaluates debtor creditworthiness in addition to seller; automated invoice submission for factoring must account for debtor approval step; automated factoring for new debtors may be declined or have lower advance rate; implement debtor pre-approval check before automated factoring workflow for new customer invoices

SAVINGS ACCOUNT WITHDRAWAL LIMIT: Bluevine savings account may have withdrawal limits (bank regulatory limits on savings account transfers); automated cash sweeping from Bluevine savings to checking for operational needs must stay within withdrawal frequency limits; automated daily cash sweeping that exceeds regulatory transfer limits creates account restriction

ACH PUSH vs PULL AUTHORIZATION REQUIREMENTS: Bluevine ACH requires proper authorization for pull transactions (debiting customer accounts); automated ACH pull for loan repayment requires ACH authorization agreement from business owner; automated loan repayment ACH without proper authorization creates NACHA R10 unauthorized return and compliance issue
agent_readiness.security_notes: SMB banking and credit. FDIC pass-through, FinCEN, BSA. OAuth2. US. Business account, credit, and payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: SMB banking and lending. FDIC pass-through, FinCEN, BSA. OAuth2. US. Account, credit, and transaction data.
